name: Rice Blast
symptoms: Spindle-shaped spots with gray centers and brown borders. Lesions can appear on leaves, nodes, and panicles. Severe infection leads to neck rot.
causes: Fungal pathogen, high humidity, cool nighttime temperatures, and excessive nitrogen fertilizer.

organic_treatment.method: Foliar spray of Neem oil or Trichoderma viride.
organic_treatment.materials: Neem oil, soap solution, Trichoderma viride formulation.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5ml Neem oil per liter of water; 5g Trichoderma per liter.

prevention: Balanced nitrogen application, use resistant varieties, and maintain proper spacing for airflow.

name: Wheat Rust
symptoms: Small, oval, orange-to-red pustules on leaves and stems. Pustules turn black as the plant matures.
causes: Airborne fungal spores, moist conditions, and temperatures between 15-25°C.

organic_treatment.method: Spray fermented butter milk or Cow urine decoction.
organic_treatment.materials: Fermented buttermilk (3-5 days old), Cow urine.
organic_treatment.dosage: 1 liter buttermilk in 10 liters water; 1:10 ratio for cow urine.

prevention: Eradicate barberry bushes (alternate host), early sowing, and resistant cultivars.

name: Tomato Late Blight
symptoms: Dark, water-soaked spots on leaves that rapidly enlarge. White fungal growth appears on the underside in humid conditions. Fruits develop firm, dark brown decay.
causes: Oomycete pathogen, cool and wet weather (high humidity > 90%).

organic_treatment.method: Baking soda spray or Copper-based organic fungicide.
organic_treatment.materials: Baking soda, liquid soap, vegetable oil.
organic_treatment.dosage: 1 tbsp baking soda, 1 tsp soap, 1 tbsp oil in 4 liters water.

prevention: Crop rotation, avoid overhead irrigation, and ensure good drainage.

name: Cotton Bollworm
symptoms: Larvae bore into squares, flowers, and bolls. Entry holes are visible, often surrounded by fecal matter. Significant yield loss due to boll damage.
causes: Pest infestation, migration from neighboring crops, favorable temperatures.

organic_treatment.method: Neem Seed Kernel Extract (NSKE) or BT spray.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5% NSKE; 2g BT per liter of water.

prevention: Pheromone traps (5 per acre), bird perches, and intercropping with cowpea or maize.

name: Maize Downy Mildew
symptoms: Yellowish-green streaks on leaves. White downy growth on both surfaces. Stunted growth and 'crazy top' (distorted tassel).
causes: Soil-borne and seed-borne fungus, high humidity, waterlogging.

organic_treatment.method: Seed treatment with Trichoderma and foliar spray of Ginger-Garlic-Chili extract.
organic_treatment.materials: Ginger, Garlic, Green Chili, Trichoderma viride.
organic_treatment.dosage: 10g Trichoderma per kg seed; 2% GGC extract.

prevention: Early planting, balanced fertilization, and removal of infected plants.

name: Potato Early Blight
symptoms: Small, dark, circular spots with concentric rings (target-like appearance) on older leaves. Lower leaves turn yellow and drop.
causes: Fungal pathogen, alternating wet and dry periods, high temperatures (24-29°C).

organic_treatment.method: Compost tea spray or milk-water solution.
organic_treatment.dosage: 1:5 ratio for compost tea; 1:9 ratio for milk-water.

prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds, avoid water stress, and rotate with non-solanaceous crops.

name: Sugarcane Red Rot
symptoms: Reddish discoloration of internal tissues with white cross-bands. Withering of leaves and acidic smell in split canes.
causes: Infected setts, irrigation water carrying spores, high soil moisture.

organic_treatment.method: Sett treatment with Trichoderma and soil application.
organic_treatment.dosage: 10g Trichoderma per liter for sett soaking; 2kg per acre in soil.

prevention: Hot water treatment of setts (50°C for 2 hours), field sanitation, and 2-year crop rotation.

name: Mango Anthracnose
symptoms: Black, sunken spots on leaves, twigs, and fruits. Young leaves wither and drop. Fruits rot during ripening.
causes: Fungal pathogen, high humidity, frequent rains during flowering.

organic_treatment.method: Spray of Pseudomonas fluorescens or Neem oil.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5g Pseudomonas per liter; 3ml Neem oil per liter.

prevention: Pruning of dead twigs, post-harvest hot water treatment of fruit (52°C for 5 min).

name: Banana Bunchy Top
symptoms: 'Morse code' (dots and dashes) on leaf veins. New leaves are narrow, upright, and bunched at the top. Severe stunting.
causes: Virus transmitted by the banana aphid (Pentalonia nigronervosa).

organic_treatment.method: Control aphids with Neem oil and destroy infected plants.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5ml Neem oil per liter of water.

prevention: Use virus-free suckers/tissue culture plants, rogueing (removing) infected plants immediately.

name: Chili Leaf Curl
symptoms: Leaves curl upwards and inwards, thicken, and become brittle. Stunted plants with few small fruits.
causes: Virus transmitted by Whiteflies (Bemisia tabaci).

organic_treatment.method: Spray Agni Astra or yellow sticky traps for whiteflies.
organic_treatment.materials: Cow urine, tobacco, chili, garlic decoction (Agni Astra).
organic_treatment.dosage: 250ml Agni Astra in 15 liters of water.

prevention: Border cropping with maize or sorghum, using silver-colored mulches.

name: Groundnut Leaf Spot
symptoms: Circular dark brown spots with yellow halos on leaves. In later stages, leaves drop prematurely.
causes: Fungal pathogen, high humidity, high leaf wetness.

organic_treatment.method: Foliar spray of Dashparni Ark.
organic_treatment.materials: Fermented extract of 10 types of leaves in cow urine.
organic_treatment.dosage: 500ml Dashparni Ark in 15 liters of water.

prevention: Deep summer plowing, balanced potash application, and removal of plant debris.

name: Citrus Canker
symptoms: Raised, corky, brown lesions on leaves, stems, and fruits. Lesions are surrounded by yellow halos.
causes: Bacterial pathogen, wind-driven rain, overhead irrigation.

organic_treatment.method: Spray of Streptomycin mixed with Copper oxychloride or cow dung slurry.
organic_treatment.materials: Streptomycin sulfate, Copper oxychloride, Fresh cow dung.
organic_treatment.dosage: 0.1g Streptomycin + 3g Copper oxychloride per liter.

prevention: Pruning infected parts during the dry season, using resistant rootstocks like Rangpur lime.

name: Pigeon Pea Wilt
symptoms: Gradual yellowing and withering of leaves. Blackening of the main root and stem pith (vascular browning).
causes: Soil-borne fungus, continuous cropping of pigeon pea.

organic_treatment.method: Soil application of Trichoderma viride enriched Farm Yard Manure (FYM).
organic_treatment.dosage: 2kg Trichoderma mixed with 100kg FYM per acre.

prevention: Intercropping with Sorghum, 3-year crop rotation, and deep summer plowing.

name: Grapes Downy Mildew
symptoms: Yellowish 'oil spots' on the upper leaf surface. White cottony growth on the underside. Infected berries turn brown and shrivel.
causes: Fungal-like oomycete, high humidity (>85%), rainfall, and leaf wetness.

organic_treatment.method: Bordeaux mixture spray (1%).
organic_treatment.dosage: 1kg Copper sulfate + 1kg Lime in 100 liters water.

prevention: Proper canopy management for ventilation, removal of fallen leaves, and drip irrigation.

name: Onion Purple Blotch
symptoms: Small, water-soaked lesions that turn purple with a yellowish border. Leaves break and fall.
causes: Fungal pathogen, warm and humid weather (25-30°C).

organic_treatment.method: Spray of Neem oil or Jeevamrutha.
organic_treatment.materials: Neem oil, Jeevamrutha (cow dung/urine/jaggery/pulse flour fermented).
organic_treatment.dosage: 5ml Neem oil per liter; 1 liter Jeevamrutha in 10 liters water.

prevention: Maintain wider spacing, avoid excessive nitrogen, and improve drainage.

name: Apple Scab
symptoms: Olive-green to black velvety spots on leaves and fruit. Fruit becomes cracked and deformed.
causes: Fungal pathogen, cool and wet spring weather.

organic_treatment.method: Sulfur-based organic fungicide or Apple Cider Vinegar spray.
organic_treatment.dosage: 3g Sulfur per liter; 1 tbsp vinegar in 1 liter water.

prevention: Pruning for sunlight penetration, removal of fallen leaves in winter.

name: Papaya Ring Spot
symptoms: Yellowing and mottling of leaves. Dark green streaks on petioles. Fruits show characteristic 'ring spots'.
causes: Virus transmitted by aphids.

organic_treatment.method: Aphid management with Neem oil and border cropping with corn.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5ml Neem oil per liter; 3 rows of corn as border.

prevention: Rogueing of infected plants, using net houses for nursery.

name: Soybean Rust
symptoms: Tan to reddish-brown lesions on the underside of leaves. Severe infection causes premature defoliation.
causes: Airborne spores, high humidity, long periods of leaf wetness.

organic_treatment.method: Spray of fermented wood ash or Ginger extract.
organic_treatment.dosage: 20g Wood ash per liter; 5% Ginger extract.

prevention: Early planting, monitoring for early signs, and choosing resistant varieties.

name: Coffee Leaf Rust
symptoms: Yellow-orange powdery spots on the underside of leaves. Premature leaf drop and reduced bean quality.
causes: Fungal pathogen, humidity, rainfall, and moderate temperatures.

organic_treatment.method: Spray of Bacillus subtilis or 0.5% Bordeaux mixture.
organic_treatment.materials: Bacillus subtilis formulation, Copper sulfate, Lime.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5g Bacillus per liter; 0.5kg Cu + 0.5kg Lime per 100L water.

prevention: Proper shading, balanced fertilization, and pruning.

name: Okra Yellow Vein Mosaic
symptoms: Veins turn yellow, while the rest of the leaf remains green. Fruits are small, yellowish-white, and tough.
causes: Virus transmitted by whiteflies.

organic_treatment.method: Management of whiteflies with Neem oil or Verticillium lecanii.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5ml Neem oil per liter; 5g Verticillium per liter.

prevention: Removing alternative weed hosts, sowing resistant hybrids like 'Arka Anamika'.

name: Mustard Alternaria Blight
symptoms: Small, black circular spots on leaves, stems, and siliquae. Spots enlarge with concentric rings.
causes: Fungal pathogen, cool and moist weather, late sowing.

organic_treatment.method: Seed treatment with garlic extract and foliar spray of Trichoderma.
organic_treatment.dosage: 2% Garlic extract for seeds; 5g Trichoderma per liter spray.

prevention: Timely sowing (October), removal of crop residues, and balanced fertilization.

name: Turmeric Leaf Spot
symptoms: Oblong spots with yellow halos on leaves. Centers turn gray and thin out, sometimes causing holes.
causes: Fungal pathogen, high humidity, frequent rainfall.

organic_treatment.method: Foliar spray of Pseudomonas fluorescens.
organic_treatment.dosage: 10g per liter of water.

prevention: Selection of healthy rhizomes, crop rotation, and avoidance of shade.

name: Brinjal Phomopsis Blight
symptoms: Circular brown spots on leaves; dark, sunken spots on fruits that lead to soft rot.
causes: Infected seeds, fungal spores spreading through rain splashes.

organic_treatment.method: Seed treatment and foliar spray with Neem Seed Kernel Extract.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5% NSKE solution.

prevention: Use disease-free seeds, long crop rotation, and removal of diseased plant parts.

name: Cabbage Black Rot
symptoms: Yellow, V-shaped lesions starting from leaf margins. Veins within lesions turn black.
causes: Bacterial pathogen, warm and wet conditions, infected seeds/seedlings.

organic_treatment.method: Seed treatment with hot water and foliar spray of Serenade (Bacillus subtilis).
organic_treatment.dosage: Hot water (50°C) for 30 min; 5ml Serenade per liter.

prevention: Use certified seeds, 3-year crop rotation (no crucifers), and improve soil drainage.

name: Coconut Root Wilt
symptoms: Flaccidity (drooping) of leaflets. Yellowing and marginal necrosis of older leaves. Reduced nut size.
causes: Phytoplasma transmitted by Lace wing bug and Plant hopper.

organic_treatment.method: Basal application of Neem cake and green manuring.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5kg Neem cake per palm per year.

prevention: Integrated nutrient management, planting tolerant varieties like 'Chowghat Orange Dwarf'.

name: Tobacco Mosaic
symptoms: Mottling, light and dark green patches on leaves. Stunting and leaf distortion.
causes: Virus spread through mechanical contact (hands, tools).

organic_treatment.method: Wash hands/tools with soap; spray milk solution.
organic_treatment.dosage: 1:10 ratio of milk to water.

prevention: Strict sanitation, avoid smoking near plants, use resistant varieties.

name: Ginger Rhizome Rot
symptoms: Yellowing of leaves, stunting, and water-soaked lesions at the base of the pseudostem. Rhizomes become soft and rot.
causes: Oomycete pathogen, waterlogging, contaminated rhizomes.

organic_treatment.method: Rhizome treatment with Trichoderma viride and soil drenching.
organic_treatment.dosage: 10g Trichoderma per liter of water.

prevention: Selection of healthy seeds, solarization of beds, and proper drainage.

name: Pea Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White, powdery patches on leaves, stems, and pods. Leaves turn yellow and brown.
causes: Fungal pathogen, dry weather with high humidity at night.

organic_treatment.method: Spray of wettable sulfur or Neem oil.
organic_treatment.dosage: 3g Sulfur per liter; 5ml Neem oil per liter.

prevention: Early sowing, use of resistant varieties, and avoiding late irrigation.

name: Sunflower Downy Mildew
symptoms: Systemic stunting, chlorosis (yellowing) along leaf veins, and white fungal growth on the underside.
causes: Soil-borne and seed-borne oomycete, cool and wet soil conditions.

organic_treatment.method: Seed treatment with bio-control agents like Pseudomonas fluorescens.
organic_treatment.dosage: 10g per kg of seed.

prevention: 3-year crop rotation, deep plowing, and avoiding waterlogged fields.

name: Black Pepper Foot Rot
symptoms: Dark, water-soaked lesions on the collar region. Leaves turn yellow and drop; vine wilts rapidly.
causes: Oomycete pathogen, heavy monsoon rains, poor drainage.

organic_treatment.method: Application of Trichoderma harzianum and 1% Bordeaux mixture to the base.
organic_treatment.dosage: 50g Trichoderma in 5kg FYM per vine.

prevention: Providing proper drainage, avoiding physical injury to the root zone, and rogueing infected vines.

name: Castor Gray Mold
symptoms: Grayish fungal growth on the spikes. Bolls rot and drop prematurely.
causes: Continuous rain or high humidity during the flowering stage.

organic_treatment.method: Foliar spray of Trichoderma viride or Garlic extract.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5g Trichoderma per liter.

prevention: Removal of infected spikes, avoiding excessive irrigation during flowering.

name: Jute Stem Rot
symptoms: Black, sunken lesions on the stem near the soil line. Stem breaks easily; black pycnidia (dots) visible in lesions.
causes: Soil-borne fungus, high temperature, and moisture stress.

organic_treatment.method: Soil application of Neem cake and seed treatment with Trichoderma.
organic_treatment.dosage: 250kg Neem cake per acre.

prevention: Liming of acidic soils, crop rotation with paddy, and balanced NPK.

name: Cardamom Azhukal (Fruit Rot)
symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on leaves and berries. Berries rot and drop; leaves decay.
causes: Fungal pathogen, heavy rainfall, high humidity, and thick shade.

organic_treatment.method: Spray and drench with 1% Bordeaux mixture.
organic_treatment.dosage: 1kg each per 100 liters water.

prevention: Regulating shade, providing drainage, and cleaning the plant base before monsoon.

name: Rose Dieback
symptoms: Blackening of stems starting from the pruned tip downwards. Stem becomes shriveled and dry.
causes: Fungal infection through pruning wounds, nutrient deficiency.

organic_treatment.method: Apply Cow dung paste with Copper oxychloride to pruned tips.
organic_treatment.dosage: Paste consistency.

prevention: Pruning at a 45-degree angle, using sharp and sanitized tools, and balanced fertilization.

name: Cucumber Mosaic
symptoms: Mosaic patterns of light and dark green on leaves. Leaves become small and distorted. Fruits are 'warty' and bitter.
causes: Virus transmitted by aphids.

organic_treatment.method: Aphid control with Neem oil; use of yellow sticky traps.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5ml Neem oil per liter.

chemical_treatment.product: None for virus; Acetamiprid for aphids.

prevention: Eliminating weed hosts, using reflective mulches.

name: Chillies Anthracnose (Fruit Rot)
symptoms: Circular black, sunken spots on fruits with concentric rings. Fruits dry up and shrivel.
causes: Seed-borne and air-borne fungus, high humidity.

organic_treatment.method: Seed treatment with Pseudomonas and spray of Neem oil.
organic_treatment.dosage: 10g Pseudomonas/kg seed; 5ml Neem oil per liter.

prevention: Use healthy seeds, remove infected fruits, and avoid overhead irrigation.

name: Wheat Loose Smut
symptoms: Ear heads emerge early; grains are replaced by black powdery mass of spores.
causes: Internally seed-borne fungus.

organic_treatment.method: Solar seed treatment (soaking seeds in water and then drying in hot sun).
organic_treatment.dosage: Soak for 4 hours; dry on floor for 4 hours in May-June.

prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds and roguing of infected ears.

name: Paddy Bacterial Leaf Blight
symptoms: Yellowish-white wavy lesions starting from leaf tips and margins. Droplets of bacterial ooze (amber colored) may appear.
causes: Bacterial pathogen, high nitrogen, heavy rain, and winds.

organic_treatment.method: Spray of 20% Cow dung slurry or Streptocycline.
organic_treatment.dosage: 20kg dung in 100L water (filter before spray); 0.1g Strepto/L.

prevention: Avoid flooding, reduce nitrogen application in infected fields, and use resistant varieties like 'BPT 5204'.

name: Groundnut Bud Necrosis
symptoms: Necrotic spots and rings on young leaves. Terminal bud withers and dies. Plants are stunted and bushy.
causes: Virus transmitted by Thrips (Frankliniella schultzei).

organic_treatment.method: Control thrips with Neem oil or Beauveria bassiana.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5ml Neem oil/L; 5g Beauveria/L.

prevention: Timely sowing, intercropping with Pearl millet, and high seeding rate.

name: Cotton Bacterial Blight
symptoms: Angular, water-soaked spots on leaves. 'Black arm' phase on stems and 'Boll rot' phase on bolls.
causes: Bacterial pathogen, high humidity, and warm weather.

organic_treatment.method: Seed treatment with acid delinting and foliar spray of Pseudomonas.
organic_treatment.dosage: 10g Pseudomonas per liter.

prevention: Removal of crop stalks, using resistant varieties, and seed treatment.

name: Potato Soft Rot
symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on tubers that rapidly turn into soft, mushy, foul-smelling mass.
causes: Bacterial pathogen, high soil moisture, poor storage ventilation.

organic_treatment.method: Drying tubers in shade and ensuring proper storage ventilation.
organic_treatment.dosage: N/A

prevention: Avoid bruising during harvest, store in cool and dry conditions, and improve field drainage.

name: Citrus Greening (HLB)
symptoms: Mottled yellowing of leaves (blotchy mottle). Fruits are small, lopsided, and remain green at the bottom. Bitter juice.
causes: Bacteria transmitted by Citrus Psyllid (Diaphorina citri).

organic_treatment.method: Psyllid control with Neem oil and integrated nutrient management.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5ml Neem oil/L.

prevention: Use of disease-free nursery plants, rigorous control of the vector psyllid.

name: Mango Malformation
symptoms: Bunchy appearance of vegetative shoots and floral panicles ('Witch's broom'). Flowers fail to set fruit.
causes: Fungal pathogen, mite association (Aceria mangiferae).

organic_treatment.method: Pruning of malformed parts and spray of Neem oil for mites.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5ml per liter.

prevention: Removal and burning of malformed panicles, proper pruning.

name: Banana Wilt (Panama Disease)
symptoms: Yellowing of leaf margins of older leaves. Pseudostem splitting and reddish-brown discoloration of the rhizome.
causes: Soil-borne fungus, infected suckers.

organic_treatment.method: Corm injection and soil drenching with Pseudomonas fluorescens.
organic_treatment.dosage: 3g Pseudomonas in 5ml water for corm injection.

prevention: Using resistant varieties like 'Grand Naine', flood fallowing, and crop rotation with paddy.

name: Coconut Bud Rot
symptoms: Yellowing of the youngest leaf (spear leaf), which later withers and falls. The bud rots and emits a foul smell.
causes: Fungal pathogen, high humidity, and heavy rainfall.

organic_treatment.method: Removal of dead tissues and application of Bordeaux paste.
organic_treatment.dosage: Paste consistency.

prevention: Prophylactic spray of 1% Bordeaux mixture before monsoon, cleaning the crown.

name: Sugarcane Smut
symptoms: Production of a black, whip-like structure from the terminal bud. Stunted growth and thin canes.
causes: Infected setts, wind-borne spores.

organic_treatment.method: Rogueing and burning of infected whips; sett treatment with Trichoderma.
organic_treatment.dosage: 10g per liter of water.

prevention: Selection of smut-free setts, hot water treatment, and resistant varieties.

name: Tea Blister Blight
symptoms: Transparent or pale-yellow spots on leaves. Spots enlarge and form 'blisters' with white powdery growth on the underside.
causes: Fungal pathogen, high humidity (>90%), mist, and cool weather.

organic_treatment.method: Spray of 0.1% Copper oxychloride mixed with Nickel chloride.
organic_treatment.dosage: 2g Copper + 1g Nickel per liter.

prevention: Removal of overhanging shade trees, timely pruning, and avoiding plucking during heavy mist.

name: Guava Wilt
symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of leaves. Premature shedding and death of branches, eventually leading to the death of the tree.
causes: Soil-borne fungus, alkaline soil, poor drainage.

organic_treatment.method: Soil application of Neem cake and Trichoderma enrichment.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5kg Neem cake + 50g Trichoderma per tree.

prevention: Maintain soil pH (6.5), avoid root injury, and provide adequate drainage.

name: Coffee Berry Disease
symptoms: Dark, sunken spots on green berries. Berries shrivel, turn black, and drop.
causes: Fungal pathogen, high altitude, high humidity, and cool temperatures.

organic_treatment.method: Spray of 1% Bordeaux mixture.
organic_treatment.dosage: 1kg each per 100 liters water.

prevention: Pruning for better air circulation, removal of 'mummified' berries from the ground.

name: Papaya Mealybug
symptoms: White waxy mass on leaves, fruits, and stems. Distorted leaves and black sooty mold growth.
causes: Insect pest infestation, spread by ants.

organic_treatment.method: Release of Acerophagus papayae (parasitoid) or Neem oil spray.
organic_treatment.dosage: 5ml Neem oil per liter.

prevention: Destruction of alternative weed hosts, controlling ants with grease bands on the trunk.
